title: Violent Crime Control and Law Enforcement Act of 1994

sources.source_title: Provisions Implementing the Violent Crime Control and Law Enforcement Act of 1994
sources.notes: National Archives CFR subject page listing implementing regulations for provisions of the Violent Crime Control and Law Enforcement Act of 1994.

sources.source_title: Fact Sheet on Building Safer Communities
sources.notes: Archived White House fact sheet describing major provisions of the 1994 Crime Bill, including policing, prisons, and prevention funding.
Archived White House summary framing the 1994 law as a major federal anti-crime package and describing its policy scope.

sources.publisher: Cornell Law School Legal Information Institute
sources.notes: Legal summary explaining the Prison Litigation Reform Act's limits on prisoner lawsuits, exhaustion requirements, and filing restrictions.

sources.source_title: Prison Litigation Reform Act: A Critical Review
sources.notes: Review of the PLRA's practical impact on prison-condition litigation and barriers to court access.

sources.publisher: Cornell Law School Legal Information Institute
sources.notes: Statutory text page for the PLRA exhaustion requirement and related prisoner litigation provisions.

sources.source_title: Department of Justice Report on Efforts to Fully Implement the Provisions and Intent of the First Step Act
sources.notes: DOJ implementation report describing the First Step Act as a bipartisan law to improve criminal justice outcomes and reduce the federal prison population while maintaining public safety.

sources.source_title: Justice Department Announces New Rule Implementing the Federal Time Credits Program Established by the First Step Act
sources.notes: DOJ press release explaining the earned time credit program created by the First Step Act.

sources.source_title: Audit of the Federal Bureau of Prisons' Efforts to Place Inmates Close to Home
sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Justice Office of the Inspector General
sources.notes: DOJ OIG audit discussing implementation of the First Step Act and its prison placement provisions.

metrics.metric_name: Earned time credits available under the First Step Act
metrics.methodology_note: DOJ states that eligible inmates can earn 10 to 15 days of time credits for every 30 days of successful participation in qualifying programs under the First Step Act.

title: Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act

sources.source_title: Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF): 26 Years of Progress
sources.notes: Federal overview of TANF, the cash assistance structure created by the 1996 welfare reform law.

sources.source_title: Welfare Reform and the Safety Net
sources.publisher: Center on Budget and Policy Priorities
sources.notes: Policy analysis summarizing how welfare reform changed access to cash assistance and weakened parts of the safety net.

sources.source_title: Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act of 1996
sources.notes: Federal reference page for the 1996 welfare reform law.

sources.source_title: The Affordable Care Act and African Americans
sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
sources.notes: HHS overview of ACA provisions expected to improve health coverage and access for African Americans.

sources.source_title: Health Insurance Coverage and Access to Care Among Black Americans: Recent Trends and Key Challenges
sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
sources.notes: HHS issue brief stating that since implementation of the ACA's coverage provisions, the uninsured rate among nonelderly Black Americans decreased by 10 percentage points.

sources.source_title: The Affordable Care Act and Its Accomplishments
sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
sources.notes: HHS briefing book stating that Medicaid expansions helped narrow racial disparities in health insurance coverage, especially for Black and Latino Americans.

metrics.metric_name: Change in uninsured rate among nonelderly Black Americans after ACA coverage provisions
metrics.methodology_note: HHS reported in 2024 that since implementation of the Affordable Care Act coverage provisions, the uninsured rate among nonelderly Black Americans decreased by 10 percentage points.
